July 22. 1975
. a 7- (15/
carl Olsen
,6) 7‘
San Parnando Valley
.
young Republicans
P.O. Box 1531
studio city, California 91604
pear Mr. Olson:
This letter confirms our telephone conversation or
July 19, 1975, concerning the potential reporting obligations
or the San Fernando Valley Young Republicans.
You indicated
that the club has raised approximately $1,000.
or this
amount, Approximately $500 is profit realized from the state
convention, another $500 was obtained from dues, and the
remaining $50 or so was raised from club parties.
You have
mace no payments to candidates or camitteea supporting
candidates or ballot measures in this calendar year.
This club will become a "committee" within the meaning
or the Political Reform Act if it collects $500 or more in
contrioutions in a calendar year.
Gov. Code Section 82013
(a).
The term "contributions" includes only those receipts
that are "earmarked" for political purposes, that is,
payments that the donor knows or has reason to know will he
ﬂed by the club to make political expenditures and contribu-
tions.
Thus. dues payments are not contriouticns unless the
club routinely uses all or a portion of the dues to support
candidates or measures, in which case the member would know
or have reason to knuw that some of the dues payments will be
used for political purposes.
As I understand the facts, the
san Fernando Valley Young Republican: Club does not have any
established policy of making contributlcns to candidates the
docs are not contributions received.
arguably the proceeds
from the state convention night be considered as contributions
received. but once again, you indicated that the club has not
decided what to do with those funds and may not use the funds
in: campaign activities.
Consequently, the club has not
raised $500 or more in contributions, and has no reporting
obligations at the present time.


'
Carl olson
July 22, 1975
Page Two
although payments may have been made to the club with
some expectation that they will be used for political
purposes, it may be difficult for you to ascertain when
»
soon in contributions have been received.
If so. you
should, keep track of disbursements by the club.
The club
may make contributions to candidates, measures, or official
committees of the Republican party, such as the state
central committee, county central committees, and assembly
district committees.
In addition, the club may make
expenditures in support of candidates or measures such as
printing posters or placing newspaper ads.
If the contribu-
tions and expenditures aggregate $500 in a calendar year; you
will know that the club received 5500 in contributions to
support these disbursements, and that the club is a committee.
As I indicated to you, the costsoof printing and distribur
ting a regularly published newsletter are not "expenditures"
as long as no additional costs are incurred because the
newsletter is substantially altered in style, size or format.
I hope this information will he of assistance.
Please
contact me if any of the above information is confusing or
unclear.
sincerely.
Natalie 2. West
Staff Counsel
"Evilav

sections.question_synthetic: Does the San Fernando Valley Young Republicans Club have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act based on their fundraising activities of approximately $1,000 from state convention proceeds, dues, and club parties?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The club has not raised $500 or more in contributions and has no reporting obligations at the present time, because contributions are limited to earmarked funds donors know will be used for political purposes, and the club has no established policy of using dues or convention proceeds for political expenditures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear factual background and legal analysis typical of FPPC advice letters. Date discrepancy noted (letter dated July 22, 1975 but references July 19 telephone conversation). Some OCR errors present ('mace' for 'made', 'contriouticns' for 'contributions') but content is substantially clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the San Fernando Valley Young Republicans Club have reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act based on their fundraising activities of approximately $1,000 from state convention proceeds, dues, and club parties?

CONCLUSION: The club has not raised $500 or more in contributions and has no reporting obligations at the present time, because contributions are limited to earmarked funds donors know will be used for political purposes, and the club has no established policy of using dues or convention proceeds for political expenditures.

You indicated that the club has raised approximately $1,000. or this amount, Approximately $500 is profit realized from the state convention, another $500 was obtained from dues, and the remaining $50 or so was raised from club parties. You have mace no payments to candidates or camitteea supporting candidates or ballot measures in this calendar year. This club will become a "committee" within the meaning or the Political Reform Act if it collects $500 or more in contrioutions in a calendar year. Gov. Code Section 82013 (a). The term "contributions" includes only those receipts that are "earmarked" for political purposes, that is, payments that the donor knows or has reason to know will he ﬂed by the club to make political expenditures and contribu- tions. Thus. dues payments are not contriouticns unless the club routinely uses all or a portion of the dues to support candidates or measures, in which case the member would know or have reason to knuw that some of the dues payments will be used for political purposes. As I understand the facts, the san Fernando Valley Young Republican: Club does not have any established policy of making contributlcns to candidates the docs are not contributions received. arguably the proceeds from the state convention night be considered as contributions received. but once again, you indicated that the club has not decided what to do with those funds and may not use the funds in: campaign activities. Consequently, the club has not raised $500 or more in contributions, and has no reporting obligations at the present time. ' Carl olson July 22, 1975 Page Two although payments may have been made to the club with some expectation that they will be used for political purposes, it may be difficult for you to ascertain when » soon in contributions have been received. If so. you should, keep track of disbursements by the club. The club may make contributions to candidates, measures, or official committees of the Republican party, such as the state central committee, county central committees, and assembly district committees. In addition, the club may make expenditures in support of candidates or measures such as printing posters or placing newspaper ads. If the contribu- tions and expenditures aggregate $500 in a calendar year; you will know that the club received 5500 in contributions to support these disbursements, and that the club is a committee. embedding.summary: FPPC counsel advises that the San Fernando Valley Young Republicans Club does not currently meet the $500 contribution threshold to qualify as a committee under the Political Reform Act, based on the club's lack of an established policy directing dues or convention proceeds to political purposes.
